as_of_note: Verified against official docs as fetched on the dates below. Docs change.
notes: Headline platform: capped at needs_human. Named-on-a-slide claims require human sign-off, not tool self-certification.
Additionally, 2 required step(s) are not backed by a quoted official-doc excerpt.

steps.action: Open the Netlify documentation home and select Create a new project.

evidence.excerpt: Netlify documentation index, see llms.txt . Markdown versions of this page are available by appending .md to the URL. More flexibility and credits now available for Pro plans 🎉 Netlify Documentation Build & deploy faster with any modern frontend stack or AI agent. Create a new project
project Start

steps.action: Prepare the local project folder to deploy.
steps.success_signal: The folder is unzipped and includes the project files; for anonymous publishing it already contains built files.

evidence.excerpt: project? You can drag and drop your updated project folder into the Deploy zone to deploy your updated web project. Start from zip files Section titled “Start from zip files” You can programmatically publish your app/site by using the Netlify API . Learn more about deploying a zip file to a production site . Start from local

already built your project with a site or app builder like Bolt , you can deploy and manage your project on Netlify by claiming your deployment or downloading your project files into a folder and then publishing

steps.action: Log in to the Netlify account and select the team that should manage the project.
steps.success_signal: The team-owned Projects page or Netlify Drop publisher can be opened.

evidence.excerpt: project from scratch with the help of an AI agent directly from your Netlify dashboard. To use this feature, you need a Credit-based pricing plan and must have enabled AI features . To start a new project with an AI agent: Go to your Netlify team dashboard and select Add new project or log in to your Netlify account

Netlify account at https://app.netlify.com/login . Make sure you're signed in to the Netlify team where you want to manage your project. Go to https://app.netlify.com/drop or scroll to the bottom of your team dashboard where all your team-owned projects

steps.action: Open the Netlify Drop publisher or scroll to the drag-and-drop section at the bottom of the team's Projects page.
steps.success_signal: The folder drop target is ready.

evidence.excerpt: team where you want your site project to be on Netlify. You can create a new site by dragging a project folder containing your site files (like .html files) to Netlify Drop . You can also find a drag and drop section at the bottom of your team's Projects page
drop your project folder 📁 Ready

steps.action: Drag the project folder into the Deploy zone.
steps.success_signal: The upload and deploy process begins.
notes: No literal official-doc excerpt supports the step's success_signal.

evidence.excerpt: deploy your web project. 🎉 When you connect your Git repository to Netlify, future Git push updates to your repository will automatically trigger new deployments. ← Try a different option Drag and drop your project folder 📁 Ready to deploy? Go to the Drag and Drop publisher page and drag your project folder to the Deploy zone
Deploy

steps.action: For a logged-in source folder, detect the framework, run the build if needed, and publish the result; for prebuilt output, publish the supplied files.
steps.success_signal: The project dashboard shows a Preview URL ending in netlify.app.

evidence.excerpt: build your project first, though. As long as you’re logged in, you can drag the folder for a web project that still needs a build step, and Netlify will detect your framework, run the build, and publish the result for you. If you drop a project without being logged in, Netlify publishes the files

project. Go to https://app.netlify.com/drop or scroll to the bottom of your team dashboard where all your team-owned projects will be listed. Drag and drop your folder to the Drop zone in the Netlify UI. Find your site published at the Preview URL ending in netlify.app

steps.action: Open the Preview URL shown in the project dashboard.
steps.success_signal: The intended project is visible at the Preview URL and the official guide says it is now published on the web.

evidence.excerpt: project. Go to https://app.netlify.com/drop or scroll to the bottom of your team dashboard where all your team-owned projects will be listed. Drag and drop your folder to the Drop zone in the Netlify UI. Find your site published at the Preview URL

now be published on the web so you can share it with others. 🎉 Tip If your site is not publishing or not in working order, you can investigate based on your error message or what you're finding in our troubleshooting docs .
